Hi, my name is Maria Ferrara. I'm applying to the QA Specialist position at Nova Dia and I'm responding to your email with the three questions in it. So here we go. The first question was asking what kinds of energy you look for and then how would you keep your process consistent. I would take a quick tour of the lesson. I checked the page layout for any obvious formatting issues. I'd proofread all the text. I'd review all the graphics. I'd test all the interaction. And as I was doing each of those things I'd be making notes. Finally, I'd walk through the entire lesson from start to finish referencing my notes and making sure that I caught everything that I could. The second question is talking about an experience where you caught an error and how I handled it. I reviewed an online test and one of the multiple choice questions allowed to correct answer. So I went back to the designer and pointed it out. It turns out it should have been a multiple selection question rather than a multiple choice. So she and I talked about how to rewrite it. She went into the question database. She rewrote the question. She told me it was done. I pulled it up. I retested it and everything worked out fine. Generally speaking, I had the ability to work directly with the developers. So that was a plus. I do like doing that so that I can look in their eyes and know exactly that they get what I'm saying. The last question was if you could fix things yourself versus filling out a bug ticket. So if there were something obvious like typos or some formatting thing that was really obvious and easy to correct and I had the ability to do that, I would do that myself. Something that required programming or coding and I didn't have the ability or the knowledge to do that myself, that's something that I would submit a ticket for. The best thing that I think to do in that case would be to record what I was seeing. So I would go through and record the interaction or whatever else and narrate it so that I could explain what I was seeing and why I would think it was an error. And then if I didn't have that ability to record, I'd probably create a bunch of screenshots and put arrows and notes all over them, comment them so that the developer would know exactly what to do. And I think that's all you asked for right now. Thanks very much for this opportunity.
